description: Sends a HTTP(S) request with optional mTLS (mutual TLS) client-certificate authentication. Paste certificates/keys directly or bind to application configuration variables.
label: HTTP(S) with mTLS

options.info: HTTP defines a set of request methods to indicate the desired action to be performed for the given resource.
options.label: Method
options.name: method

options.info: Sometimes you may need to pass some additional information in your request, this is possible by adding variables in your header.
options.label: Headers
options.name: headers

options.info: Enter what protocol the receiving end needs to use. mTLS only applies when HTTPS is selected.
options.label: Protocol
options.name: protocol

options.info: Enter the URL of the API that you want to use.
options.label: Url
options.name: url

options.info: Map the values that you want to use in your url
options.label: Url Parameters
options.name: urlParameters

options.info: Query parameters can be defined as the optional key-value pairs that appear after the question mark in the URL.
options.label: Query Parameters
options.name: queryParameters

options.info: An optional body containing data associated with the request.
options.label: Body
options.name: body

options.info: Map the values that you want to use in your body.
options.label: Body Parameters
options.name: bodyParameters

options.info: PEM-encoded client certificate used for mTLS authentication. Bind to an application configuration variable or a text variable containing the full certificate (including -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----- / -----END CERTIFICATE----- lines and newlines). Leave empty to disable mTLS.
options.label: Client certificate (PEM)
options.name: clientCert

options.info: PEM-encoded private key matching the client certificate. Bind to an application configuration variable or a text variable containing the full key (including header/footer lines and newlines). Required when a client certificate is provided.
options.label: Client private key (PEM)
options.name: clientKey

options.info: Optional passphrase protecting the client private key. Leave empty if the key is not encrypted.
options.label: Client key passphrase
options.name: passphrase

options.info: Optional PEM-encoded CA certificate(s) used to verify the server's certificate (for self-signed / private CAs). Bind to a configuration variable or a text variable. Leave empty to use the system trust store.
options.label: CA certificate (PEM)
options.name: caCert

options.info: When set to No, the server's TLS certificate is not verified. Use only for testing against self-signed endpoints when no CA certificate is available.
options.label: Reject unauthorized
options.name: rejectUnauthorized

options.info: The schema model can be utilized to type the output of this step when it is an object or an array of objects.
options.name: schemaModel
options.label: Schema Model

options.info: The name of the variable you want for the status code of the response.
options.name: responseCode
options.label: Response code as
